title: First Day of High School
body: Today was the first day of high school. I was so nervous that I almost threw up before getting on the bus. But it wasn't that bad. The building is huge compared to middle school. I got lost twice trying to find the chemistry lab, which was pretty embarrassing. My classes seem okay, although history looks to have a ton of reading. I made a new friend named Leo in algebra. We sat together at lunch. Hope this year goes well.

title: Good News in Algebra
body: Got my midterm algebra grade back today and I got an A! Leo got a B+, and he was pretty happy too. I was really worried about the quadratic equations section, but I guess all that extra studying paid off. We went to the diner after school to celebrate and got milkshakes. It was a good day. It's starting to get really cold outside. Winter is coming fast.

title: A New Face in History Class
body: There is a new girl in my world history class. Her name is Claire. She sat near the front and answered three questions in a row. She seems really nice, but she's also super popular already. She hangs out with the varsity crowd. I don't think she even noticed me in the back row. Oh well.

title: New School Year and Seating Charts
body: Sophomore year starts. I have English class with Claire, and thanks to the alphabetical seating chart, she sits right next to me! I was so nervous when I realized. I managed to say hi and she actually smiled and said hi back. She remembered me from history class last year! My hands were shaking a little bit when I was writing my notes. I hope I didn't look completely ridiculous.

title: Group Projects and Library Sessions
body: Mr. Davis assigned group projects in English today. He let us choose partners, and before I could even ask Leo, Claire turned to me and asked if I wanted to partner up! I was shocked. We spent two hours at the public library after school working on the outline. She has this great, bubbly laugh. We spent more time talking about music and movies than working, but it was amazing. I've really fallen for her.

title: Studying for Midterms with Claire
body: Claire asked me if I wanted to study for midterms together at the library again. We spent the whole afternoon there. She was struggling with chemistry, and since I'm pretty good at it, I helped her explain the periodic trends. She got so excited when she finally understood it. She grabbed my arm and laughed. I think my face went bright red. I really think she might feel the same way. Maybe I should ask her to the spring dance?

title: Valentine's Cowardice
body: Today was Valentine's Day. I bought a card for Claire and kept it in my backpack all day. Every time I was about to give it to her, my heart would start pounding and I'd chicken out. I saw her talking to some of the guys from the soccer team at lunch, and they were all laughing. I felt so out of place. I ended up bringing the card home. I'm such a coward. I just need to find a better time.

title: Prom is Announced
body: They announced the Spring Prom today. It's going to be on May 16th. Everyone is already talking about it and planning who they're going with. I really want to ask Claire. But the thought of asking her makes my stomach turn. What if she says no? What if she already has a date? I need to ask her before someone else does. But first, I need to make sure I look decent. Maybe I'll go suit shopping.

title: Suit Shopping with Mom
body: Mom took me to the mall today to look for a prom suit. I didn't want the standard boring black tuxedo. I wanted something modern and stylish to fit in with the cool crowd. We found this slim-fit navy blue suit. It fit me perfectly. I actually felt... confident wearing it. Mom got a bit emotional and took a picture. I hope Claire approves.

title: Dancing Practice and Getting Caught
body: I've been practicing some basic dance moves in my bedroom in front of the full-length mirror. I don't want to look foolish on the dance floor. While I was doing this weird spin, Dad opened the door to ask about dinner and just stood there staring at me. Then he burst out laughing. It was so embarrassing. He told me to 'keep practicing.' I shut the door and didn't try again today.

title: She Said Yes!
body: I DID IT! I actually asked Claire to prom! After English class, when everyone was leaving, I asked her if she had a date yet. She smiled and said no, and then I asked if she wanted to go with me. She said 'sure, that sounds fun!' I was so excited I could barely speak. I've been smiling all day. Leo keeps mocking me, but I don't care. It's going to be the best night.

title: Flowers and Limo Plans
body: Organized the flowers today. I ordered a white rose corsage for Claire and a matching boutonniere for myself. I also talked to Leo and some other guys about splitting a limo. It's costing a bit of money, but it's worth it for a perfect night. Everything is coming together. Only six days left. I'm so excited.

title: The Night Before Prom
body: It's the night before prom. I have my suit laid out on my chair, my shoes are polished, and the corsage is in the fridge. I can't sleep. My mind is just racing. I keep playing the night over in my head. I hope everything goes perfectly. I can't wait to see Claire in her dress. This is going to be a night I'll never forget.

title: Nothing
body: I can't write. Everything hurts. I'm not going back to school. I hate everyone. I just want to disappear.

title: End of Sophomore Year and Whispers
body: Sophomore year is finally over, and thank god. I barely survived the last two weeks. The video of what happened on the dance floor has been circulating on Snapchat. I see people looking at me and whispering in the hallways. Some guys in the cafeteria mocked my voice and called me 'Stinky Sam' when I walked by. I just kept my head down and walked away. I hate this place so much.

title: Deleting Social Media
body: I deleted my Instagram and Snapchat accounts today. I couldn't take the comments and the random messages anymore. Every time I opened my phone, it was just a reminder of that night. Leo tried to convince me to keep them, saying people will forget, but they won't. At least now my phone is quiet. I spend my days reading, drawing, or taking photos in the woods. It's peaceful there.

title: Trust Issues
body: I've been thinking a lot about trust. How do you know when someone is actually being nice to you, and when they're just setting you up? Claire spent months studying with me, laughing at my jokes, and talking to me. I thought she was my friend. But when it mattered, she laughed along with the guys who insulted me. She was leading them. I don't think I can ever trust anyone who acts that way again. It's safer to stay alone.

title: Senior Year and Pinned in the Library
body: Junior year starts (wait, wait, senior year? Ah, in 2020 I am a junior). Junior year starts. The halls are just as bad. I spend my lunch breaks in the back corner of the library. It's the only place where it's quiet and nobody mocks me. The librarian, Mrs. Gable, is nice. She doesn't ask questions. I just read coding books and study guides. I want to build things that don't mock you.

title: Looking at Universities Far Away
body: I started looking at colleges today. I want to go somewhere as far away as possible. Somewhere where nobody knows my name or anything about my high school years. I'm looking at the University of Toronto, University of Auckland, University of Melbourne, and ETH Zurich. If I can get into one of those, I can start fresh. I just need to study hard and get great test scores.

title: A New Year and Guarded Smiles
body: It's 2021. Another year. I still find it really hard to connect with people. Whenever someone at school smiles or tries to start a conversation, my immediate reaction is suspicion. I assume they have some hidden agenda or they're setting up a joke. I hate that I think this way, but it's how I protect myself. I just need to focus on my goals and get out of here.

title: Coding and SAT Studies
body: Spent the whole weekend coding a small music player widget. It's fun to make things work exactly the way they should. In code, there are no surprises; if there's a bug, it's logical and you can fix it. When I'm not coding, I'm doing practice math tests for the SAT. I need to make sure my score is high enough for the international scholarships.

title: A Quiet Graduation
body: Graduation day. I sat in the back row, walked up to get my diploma when my name was called, and left immediately afterward. No prom for me this year, thank goodness. I didn't want to celebrate with any of these people anyway. I said goodbye to Leo, who is going to state college. He's been a good friend. Now, I just wait for the university decisions.

title: Packing for a Fresh Start
body: I'm packing my bags. I leave for college next week. I've donated most of my old clothes and books. I want to travel light. It feels as though a massive weight is lifting off my chest. I am finally leaving this town. I have a chance to be someone new, where nobody knows anything about my past or that disastrous night. I'm ready.

title: First Month of College and New Friends
body: I've been at university for a month now. The campus is beautiful and my roommate, Marcus, is really cool. We've gone to a few club meetings together. People here are so different; they don't care about high school drama or reputations. I'm still a bit guarded and quiet, but I'm slowly trying to let my guard down. Taking it one day at a time.

title: History Term Paper Outline
body:
Topic: The Impact of the Printing Press on European Reformation.

I. Introduction
   - Thesis: The printing press accelerated the spread of ideas and decentralized religious authority.
II. Context: Europe before Gutenberg
   - Scribal culture and high cost of books.
III. The technology of moveable type
IV. Case Study: Martin Luther's 95 Theses
   - Rapid translation and distribution.
V. Conclusion

title: Physics Formula Sheet
body:
Kinematics:
- v = u + at
- s = ut + 0.5 * a * t^2
- v^2 = u^2 + 2as

Dynamics:
- F = ma
- F_friction = mu * N

Work, Energy, Power:
- W = F * d * cos(theta)
- KE = 0.5 * m * v^2
- PE = mgh
- P = W/t = F * v

title: AP Chemistry Lab Notes
body:
Experiment 4: Acid-Base Titration

Objective: Determine the molarity of an unknown HCl solution using a standard NaOH solution (0.10M).

Procedure:
1. Rinse buret with NaOH.
2. Measure 10.0 mL of HCl into Erlenmeyer flask, add 2 drops of phenolphthalein.
3. Titrate until faint pink color persists for 30s.

Data:
- Trial 1: 12.4 mL NaOH
- Trial 2: 12.2 mL NaOH
- Trial 3: 12.3 mL NaOH
- Average: 12.3 mL

Calculation: M1V1 = M2V2 -> M_HCl = (0.10 * 12.3) / 10.0 = 0.123M

title: Calculus Limit Practice Problems
body:
Must review limits at infinity and L'Hopital's Rule before the test on Thursday.

Problem 1: lim (x->3) (x^2 - 9)/(x - 3)
- Factor: (x-3)(x+3)/(x-3) = x+3 -> limit is 6.

Problem 2: lim (x->0) sin(x)/x
- Standard limit: 1.

Problem 3: lim (x->infinity) (2x^2 - 3)/(5x^2 + 4x - 1)
- Divide by x^2: 2 / 5 = 0.4.

title: English Essay Peer Review Comments
body:
Peer reviewer: Leo
My Essay: Symbolism in The Great Gatsby

Comments:
- Good introduction, but the thesis statement is a bit wordy. Try to make it punchier.
- The paragraph about the green light is really strong, but you need more direct quotes to support your claims about Gatsby's disillusionment.
- Watch out for spelling mistakes on 'desperate' and 'disillusioned' (I misspelled them about three times, oops).
- Conclusion wraps up well but could connect more to the theme of the American Dream.

title: AP Calculus Exam Registration Info
body:
Exam Date: May 4, 2021 at 8:00 AM
Location: Gymnasium
Fee: $95 (paid on portal)

Items to bring:
- Two sharpened No. 2 pencils with erasers
- Approved graphing calculator (check battery today!)
- Government-issued ID

Do not bring: phone, smart watch, scrap paper.

title: Computer Science Study Resources
body:
Useful websites for learning algorithms and data structures:
- GeeksforGeeks: great for basic concepts.
- LeetCode: practice problems for sorting and searching.
- Khan Academy: review binary search tree concepts.
- MIT OpenCourseWare: Intro to Algorithms lecture videos on YouTube.

body:
Books we are reading this semester in AP Lit:
1. Hamlet - Shakespeare
2. Frankenstein - Mary Shelley
3. Wuthering Heights - Emily Bronte
4. Death of a Salesman - Arthur Miller
5. The Invisible Man - Ralph Ellison

Need to buy copy of Frankenstein by next week.

title: Math Competition Sign Up
body:
The local high school math olympiad registration deadline is October 25.
Registration fee: free for students.
Test format: 25 multiple choice questions, 75 minutes.
Leo is signing up too. Practice sessions are held in Mr. Garcia's room on Tuesdays after school.

title: Biology Flashcards Outline
body:
Cellular Respiration:
1. Glycolysis: Cytoplasm, converts glucose to pyruvate, yields 2 ATP and 2 NADH.
2. Krebs Cycle: Mitochondria matrix, yields 2 ATP, 6 NADH, 2 FADH2.
3. Electron Transport Chain: Inner membrane, yields ~32 ATP via oxidative phosphorylation.

title: University Target List
body:
1. University of Toronto (U of T) - Canada
   - Strong Computer Science program.
   - Far away, big city.
2. ETH Zurich - Switzerland
   - World-class tech reputation.
   - Requires high grades and maybe German, need to check.
3. University of Melbourne - Australia
   - Great campus, clean city.
4. University of Auckland - New Zealand
   - Beautiful country, very friendly.

title: Application Deadlines
body:
- University of Toronto: January 15 (early consideration), February 1 (regular deadline).
- University of Melbourne: Varies, check international calendar.
- ETH Zurich: December 15 (for next autumn, early check needed).
- University of Auckland: December 8.

title: Draft Scholarship Essay
body:
Title: Finding Order in Code

Ever since I was young, I struggled to make sense of the chaotic world around me. People were unpredictable, promises were fragile, and the social structures of high school felt built on unstable ground. In contrast, computers offered a world of absolute logic and clarity. A program operates on rules; if something fails, there is a clear cause. In my studies, I hope to merge this passion for coding with human-centered applications to build tools that help people communicate securely and reliably...

title: Recommendation Letter Requests
body:
Teachers to ask for recommendation letters:
1. Mr. Henderson (English): He knows me well from AP Lit, and he read my essay draft. He said he'd be glad to write one.
2. Mrs. Gable (Librarian): Not a subject teacher, but she can write about my study habits and dedication in the library.
3. Mr. Garcia (Math): He knows my performance in the algebra and competition prep classes.

title: SAT Score Report Goals
body:
Need a minimum score of 1450 to be competitive for international merit scholarships.
Target breakdown:
- Math: 760+ (Algebra is my strength, need to avoid simple arithmetic mistakes)
- Reading & Writing: 700+ (Need to work on vocabulary and time management for the long passages)

Practice test score: 1420. Almost there.

title: U of T Portal Login Details
body:
Portal: Join U of T
Username: samparker2015@gmail.com
Password: •••••••••••• (saved in password manager)
U of T Applicant ID: 100928132

Check checklist: transcript uploaded, fee paid, counselor recommendation submitted.

title: Passport Renewal To Do
body:
My passport expires in February 2022. If I'm going abroad, most visas require it to be valid for at least 6 months after entry.
To-Do:
1. Get two passport photos at Walgreens.
2. Fill out renewal form DS-82.
3. Mail form, old passport, photos, and check ($130) via USPS.

title: ETH Zurich Requirements Check
body: Undergraduate programs at ETH Zurich are mostly conducted in German, not English. That is a massive blocker. I'd have to take a language prep course first. University of Toronto or Melbourne are much easier since the lectures are in English. I'll prioritize U of T.

title: Camera Gear Wishlist
body:
Current Gear: Canon Rebel T6 (standard kit lens 18-55mm)

Wishlist:
1. Canon EF 50mm f/1.8 STM (The 'Nifty Fifty') - great for portraits and low-light street photos. Very cheap (~$125).
2. Joby GorillaPod: flexible tripod for trail shooting.
3. SanDisk 64GB SD card (Class 10 for faster file writes).

title: Contest Submission: Golden Skies
body:
Submitted my photograph 'Golden Skies' to the National Geographic Student Contest.

Description: A landscape shot of the mountains at sunset, with warm golden rays slicing through heavy gray clouds. In this photo, I wanted to represent the contrast of light emerging from a dark, stormy sky.
Camera settings: f/8, 1/160s, ISO 200, focal length 35mm.

title: Photography Cheat Sheet
body:
Aperture (f-stop):
- Low (f/1.8, f/2.8) = shallow depth of field, blurry background (bokeh).
- High (f/8, f/11) = deep depth of field, everything in focus (landscapes).

Shutter Speed:
- Fast (1/500s, 1/1000s) = freezes action (sports, flying birds).
- Slow (1/10s, 2s) = blur motion (running water, light trails).

ISO:
- Low (100, 200) = clean, high quality.
- High (1600, 3200) = noisy, grainy (use only in dark settings).

title: Nature Shoot Locations
body:
Places to hike and take photos:
1. Pine Ridge Trail: great forest paths, tall trees.
2. Miller's Lake: go at sunrise for reflections and fog over the water.
3. Old Quarry: rugged rock faces, good textures for black & white photography.
4. Windy Hill: sunset views, wide horizon.

title: Webinar Notes: Light and Shadow
body:
Presenter: Alan Carter
Key takeaways:
- Golden hour is 1 hour after sunrise and 1 hour before sunset. The light is warm and soft.
- Blue hour is 30 minutes after sunset. Cool tones.
- Harsh mid-day sun creates ugly shadows on faces. Use a diffuser or shoot in the shade.

title: Summer Photo Project Ideas
body:
Theme: Reflections
- Rain puddles reflecting neon shop signs.
- Car side mirrors reflecting the sunset.
- Windows reflecting trees.
- Focus on symmetry and balance. Use f/4 to keep the reflection sharp while separating the foreground.

title: The Martian - Movie Review
body: Watched The Martian starring Matt Damon today. It was really good. I loved how he used science and botany to solve every problem. 'I'm going to have to science the sh*t out of this.' It's a great quote. It makes you feel as though any problem can be solved if you analyze it logically instead of panicking. 9/10.

title: Scribble on Social Media
body: We live in this weird era where everyone is performance-driven. On social media, you only show the perfect highlights of your life. But it's exhausting. If you have a bad day or you feel lonely, you can't post about it because it looks 'desperate.' So we all just pretend we're happy and popular. It feels so fake.

title: Rain on the Window
body: It's raining cats and dogs today. I'm sitting by my window watching the droplets trace paths down the glass pane. It resembles a race; one drop catches up to another, merges, and falls faster. I could watch it for hours. It's a nice distraction from all the thoughts running through my head.

title: A Book on Trust
body: Finished reading 'The Gift of Fear' by Gavin de Becker. It discusses how our intuition is a survival mechanism. He says that when we feel uneasy about someone, we shouldn't dismiss it as 'paranoia' or try to be 'polite.' Polite gets people hurt. This really resonated with me. Trust your gut. If someone feels off, keep your distance.

title: Why Code?
body: Coding is akin to building a castle out of thin air. You type these words into a file, and if you structure them correctly, a program comes to life. It can play music, display files, or calculate equations. It's the closest thing to magic that we have. And best of all, a computer never lies to you or betrays you.

title: The Sound of Silence
body: Silence is underrated. When you're constantly surrounded by noise, music, and people talking, you never hear your own thoughts. Sometimes you need to sit in a dark, silent room for 10 minutes just to check in with yourself. It's akin to resetting the system cache.

title: Interstellar - Movie Review
body: Watched Interstellar. The soundtrack by Hans Zimmer is absolutely incredible. The pipe organ music gives you goosebumps. The scene where Cooper watches the messages from his children after 23 years is heartbreaking. It's a masterpiece about time, distance, and family. 10/10.

title: Notes on Nostalgia
body: Nostalgia is a tricky thing. It makes you remember the past as warmer and happier than it actually was. You forget the anxiety, the lonely nights, and the confusion. But maybe that's a defense mechanism to help us move forward. We filter out the bad parts to keep a coherent story of ourselves.

body:
1. Learn basic git version control (essential for team projects).
2. Complete JavaScript foundations course on Coursera.
3. Build a small static webpage showing my photography portfolio.
4. Solve at least 20 simple programming problems on LeetCode.

title: The Clockmaker's Secret - Outline
body:
Genre: Sci-Fi / Steampunk mystery

Plot: An old clockmaker in London creates a mechanical pocket watch that doesn't track time, but rather tracks the distance to the owner's nearest alternate-universe self.

Characters:
- Arthur Pendelton: The eccentric clockmaker.
- Clara Vance: A young apprentice who accidentally triggers the watch.
- The Observers: A secret society trying to destroy Arthur's blueprints.

title: The Clockmaker's Secret - Chapter 1
body: Arthur's workshop smelled of oil, brass, and ancient paper. Hundreds of clocks ticked in unison, a rhythmic, deafening heartbeat that filled the small room. To Clara, it was the sound of order. But to Arthur, it was the ticking of a cage. He held the gold watch in his gnarled hands, his eyes reflecting the gears spinning inside. 'This does not measure the hours, child,' he whispered. 'It measures the gaps.'

title: Short Story Concept: The Echo Chamber
body: A story about a man who gets trapped in a room where every word he speaks echoes back to him, but each time it echoes, the tone changes. First it sounds friendly, then mocking, then threatening. He tries to stay completely silent, but his own thoughts start to echo too. It represents the psychological trap of dwelling on past embarrassments and criticisms.

title: Prom Night Disaster
body:
I am writing this from my bedroom floor. My hands are still shaking. Tonight was supposed to be the best night. I spent weeks preparing everything perfectly. I convinced my parents about my outfit. I wore a modern suit to fit in. I thought Claire wanted to go with me. I was so completely wrong.

We stood near the dance floor. Her friends walked over. I tried to make a joke. Claire looked at them and sneered. She mocked my dancing and my voice. She said I looked desperate asking her.

The guys walked up. They saw Claire leading the mockery. They joined in instantly. They tossed around old insults. They called me "Stinky Sam."

The worst part was Claire. She laughed right along with them.

Everyone around us was watching. People recorded it on their phones.

A massive lump formed in my throat.

The first tear slipped out.

One of the guys pointed it out to everyone.

I could not take it anymore.

I turned around and walked out.

I was crying openly.

I felt completely humiliated.

I walked home in the dark.

I have never felt so alone.

title: Math Homework Failures
body: I failed the math pop quiz today. Got a 4/10. I completely forgot how to do synthetic division. Mr. Garcia looked at me with this disappointed face. I need to study more. Leo offered to explain it, which was nice of him. I'm deleting this note because I don't want to see it anymore.

title: Draft Letter to Claire
body:
Dear Claire,

I wanted to write to ask why you did that. I thought we were friends. We spent the whole school year studying together, working on projects, and laughing. Did you only do that so you could set me up for a joke at prom? I don't understand what I did to deserve that. Why did you laugh with them?

(Never sent, deleting this. There's no point in asking. It won't change anything.)

title: Ugly Sunset Photos
body:
Photos from Windy Hill:
- DSC_0112.JPG: completely overexposed, clouds look white.
- DSC_0113.JPG: out of focus, shaky hands.
- DSC_0114.JPG: colors are muddy, not golden at all.

Deleting these. Waste of storage space.

title: Random Ideas - 2018
body:
A list of things I wanted to buy or do this year:
- New skateboard: decided not to get it, too dangerous for my wrists.
- Learn guitar: bought a cheap acoustic but my fingers hurt too much, gave up.
- Go to summer camp: cancelled due to lack of interest.

Old outdated ideas, deleting.
